# TEMPORARY validation workflow: run the single-host cross-socket H2H comparison
# (C++ h2h_benchmark_runner vs Python push h2h_perf_test_oss) on the TPU runner,
# only to confirm the Python bandwidth agrees with the C++ number. DELETE this
# file once the Python code is validated.
#
# This does NOT go through BAP (comparison is two-binary + diff, which doesn't fit
# BAP's one-workload->metrics model), so the linux-x86-n2-16 -> n2-32 override
# that BAP's pre/post jobs need does NOT apply here. It runs directly on the TPU
# box (2 NUMA nodes, needed for cross-socket), using the stable (non-transient)
# ml-build image and the -Wbitwise build fix that the earlier attempt was missing.

name: H2H Compare (C++ vs Python) [TEMP - delete after validation]

on:
  workflow_dispatch:   # manual only; run from the Actions tab on demand

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  h2h_compare:
    name: H2H cross-socket C++ vs Python
    runs-on: linux-x86-ct5lp-224-8tpu     # TPU box: 2 NUMA nodes + ml build env
    container:
      # Stable repo (NOT the -transient one the earlier attempt used, which can be
      # garbage-collected); same image tag BAP uses in benchmark_registry.pbtxt.
      image: us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/wyzhang-dev/quickstart-docker-repo/ml-build:939285342
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Build both binaries
        run: |
          bazel build -c opt --config=oss --config=ci \
            --copt=-Wno-error=bitwise-instead-of-logical \
            //tpu_raiden/examples/microbenchmarks:h2h_benchmark_runner \
            //tpu_raiden/benchmarks:h2h_perf_test_oss

      - name: Run C++ vs Python sweep
        run: |
          bash tpu_raiden/benchmarks/run_h2h_compare.sh | tee h2h_compare.txt

      - name: Upload comparison table
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: h2h_compare
          path: h2h_compare.txt
